title: Decoder frontier synthesis across attention, producer, and ranker
hypothesis: The next useful architecture decision should compare attention/KV memory pressure, output-projection producer service, and ranker coupling under the same shape assumptions. A synthesis pass should identify whether the immediate RTL frontier is still producer/ranker, or whether attention/KV memory hierarchy becomes dominant at larger contexts.

direct_comparison.primary_question: When measured attention/KV tile PPA, producer/ranker NoC coupling, and whole-decoder stage breakdown are compared together, which component dominates the next RTL frontier?
include: measured attention/KV tile-calibrated attention memory report
exclude: final PPA accounting for shared SRAM or NoC

expected_benefit: turns separate Layer 2 reports into a single component-ranking view
prevents over-optimizing standalone attention/KV or ranker blocks before checking whole-decoder dominance
selects the next measured RTL frontier with a clearer latency and traffic rationale
risks: the synthesis is still analytical and inherits assumptions from its three input reports
producer/ranker coupling uses existing measured ranker components but not a full producer RTL block
attention/KV memory hierarchy remains modeled rather than measured as an SRAM/NoC subsystem

required_evaluations.objective: Synthesize measured attention/KV tile-calibrated memory pressure, producer/ranker NoC coupling, and whole-decoder stage breakdown into one dominant-frontier report.

expected_result.reason: The output should identify whether the next measured RTL job should focus on producer/ranker, attention/KV memory hierarchy, or another whole-decoder bottleneck.
